channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Lally AI Academy: Build & Deploy an AI Product in 30 Days
item.description: Expanding your portfolio? Curious about Claude Code? Join a 30-day sprint where your team designs, builds, and deploys a live product using AI and professional-grade tools. Open to all majors, no coding needed. Teams of 3–6 (at least 1 Lally student for commercial orientation, and a faculty/staff mentor) receive up to $500 in AI tools, digital services, and makerspace access. Earn a certificate, practice teamwork, time management, and resource management. Start on or after April 1.
item.author: Students

item.title: Lally Excellence in Teaching Award Nominations Now Open
item.description: Recognize a Lally faculty member who made a lasting impact on your academic experience. The Lally Excellence in Teaching Award honors instructors who inspire learning, foster engaging classrooms, and go above and beyond for students. Submit a nomination by April 1 using this link: https://forms.gle/hzGhR2Wjih7NoppB7.
item.author: Students

item.title: Designing Posters: Turning Research into Visual Impact
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Need to create a poster for a symposium or conference? This workshop introduces key poster design principles to help you communicate your research clearly and effectively. Learn how to organize content, apply visual design strategies, and create engaging posters that connect with your audience. Registration preferred using the link.
item.author: Students

item.title: CRCC Job and Internship Fair
item.description: It’s not too late to secure an internship or expand your full-time search! Attend the Capital Region Career Consortium Job and Internship fair on April 8. Register via Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/905492b0-2207-4be4-abe1-8c8a4d842386/student_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 3/22-3/29
item.description: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! Physics I: Sunday, 3/22 (3-5pm) at the Success Center Thermodynamics: Sunday, 3/22 (5-7pm) at the Success Center MATH 1520 Math Methods: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) Blitman Classroom in ECAV (2nd floor) Diff Eq.: Tuesday, 3/24 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: More courses being add to Tutoring in Nason
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? Check it out: -Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm -CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm -CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm -CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: MARCH - APRIL Mercer X CLUBHOUSE WORKSHOP Events
item.description: Bring the heat with March - April Mercer X Workshops: Learn skills to Maximize Your Summer (Andrew Morrison ’87). Design an Integrated Circuit (IEEE) or a Pixie Radio with an Antenna (W2SZ). Learn from experts about cutting-edge AI technology (RPAi). Create your own custom keypad to learn PCB design and physically make it using the Mercer X Printed Circuit Board Prototyping Suite (IEEE). RSVP for these free events held by Mercer X Affiliated Clubs!
item.author: Students

item.title: Student Experience Excellence Awards
item.description: The Student Experience Excellence Awards (formerly the Graduating Student Awards) recognize RPI students who have made exceptional contributions to our community through academic achievement, athletic accomplishment, leadership, service, and/or volunteerism. We invite students, staff, and faculty to nominate individuals who exemplify excellence and have made a meaningful impact on our campus.
item.author: Community

item.title: The Future of Computing Research Collaboration (FCRC) 2026 Annual Workshop
item.description: This full-day workshop brings together RPI and IBM leadership, faculty, researchers, and students to highlight strategic priorities, research progress across the four FCRC tracks (AI Algorithms, AI HW/SW Codesign, Semiconductor Technology, and Quantum Computing), and future collaboration opportunities. Visit https://fcrc.rpi.edu/news for the agenda and registration link.
item.author: Community

item.title: Student Health Center Announces New Leadership
item.description: We are pleased to announce two new leadership changes in the Student Health Center: (1) Daniel H. Reirden, MD has been named Director of the RPI Student Health Center and will lead our student health strategy and clinical services; (2) Jewel Robinson, RN, has been appointed as the inaugural Nurse Manager and will oversee nursing operations and lead quality improvement initiatives.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Seminar: Olga Schurchkov, Wellesley College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Olga Shurchkov, who will present: “The Optimal Size of a Lie: Persuasion and Perceptions in Cheating Games” on Wednesday, March 25 at 3:30 PM in SAGE 3713.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy Colloquium: Professor Long Ju, Massachusetts Institute of Technology
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: “Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Effect in Experiment”
item.author: Community

item.title: ISSS Hosts Free Immigration Workshop by NPZ Law Group
item.description: ISSS will host a virtual workshop presented by NPZ Group (Nachman, Phulwani, Zimovcak (NPZ) Law Group, P.C.). Hear from expert immigration lawyers about the visa options after graduation, including H-1B, O-1, and much more! Online event on Tuesday, April 21, 2026 from 2:00 p.m. - 3:30 p.m. Register at: https://tinyurl.com/NPZ-SP26-immigration
item.author: Community

item.title: GameFest Returns to EMPAC on March 28
item.description: RPI’s annual celebration of innovation and creativity in game design returns to the EMPAC on Saturday, March 28 from 12:00 PM to 7:00 PM. GameFest features a showcase and competition for student-made games, with cash prizes awarded by a panel of experts from the Capital Region games industry.
item.author: Community

item.title: Mare Donohue’s Retirement Celebration
item.description: Date: Friday, March 27th, 2026 12:30-2:30 PM - After nine years of dedicated service to RPI, Mare Donohue, Manager of Client Services Support, will retire at the end of March. As the face of IT, Mare has led the Help Desk with care and commitment, positively impacting countless students, faculty, and staff. Please join us in celebrating her career on Friday, March 27, from 12:30–2:30 PM at the Heffner Alumni House. Kindly RSVP: https://forms.cloud.microsoft/r/W9WxAqbk2j
item.author: Community

item.title: Ice Floor and Refrigeration System Renovation at Houston Field House
item.description: Renovation of the Houston Field House ice floor is underway, with loud demolition work (including saw cutting) expected from March 23 for about one week, primarily during daytime hours. Expect increased noise, heavy equipment traffic, and some pedestrian and parking impacts, including restricted access near the building. The project has already completed initial deconstruction and is progressing into major removal phases.
item.author: Community

item.title: Jonsson Engineering Center (JEC) to Greene Walkway Construction Resuming – Access and Parking Impacts
item.description: Construction on the JEC–Greene walkway will resume March 23. The adjacent lower parking lot will be used for staging, resulting in limited parking, and daytime access beneath will be restricted. Beginning April 1, the walkway will be fully closed to allow for installation of new pavers, with detours in place. Additional work includes railing replacement, ramp and stair completion, concrete sealing, and landscaping. The project is on track for completion ahead of Commencement on May 16.
item.author: Community

item.title: Celebrate the Future Generations of Game Designers at GameFest 2026
item.description: GameFest returns to EMPAC on Saturday, March 28 from 12pm-7pm. GameFest is an annual showcase of student talent in game development, bringing together students from around New York State and the Northeast to exhibit their games. This year’s event will feature talks and film screenings from visiting artist, competitive eSports matches, and a retro gaming lounge.
item.author: Community

item.title: Sports Rules and Musicals: A Conversation with Artist Ericka Beckman
item.description: Engaging live-action choreographed sequences, as well as handcrafted layered animation, and face paced soundtrack, Beckman transforms the cultural norms communicated through common-place material such as fairytales and games. As part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” programming and Arts’ iEar Presents, Beckman will give a talk on her work and the relationship between her art and games from 12pm-2pm on March 28 at EMPAC Theater.
item.author: Community

item.title: “Maximize Your Summer” with Andrew Morrison ’87
item.description: Date: Monday, March 23, 2026 5:00 AM - 8:00 AM - “Maximize Your Summer” with Andrew Morrison ’87, Turn Your passion & Skills Into Income, Impact, and Opportunity.
item.author: Community

item.title: Fair Game Films: A Selected Screening of the Films of Ericka Beckman
item.description: As a part of the Department of Games and Experiential Media’s “Gamefest” and Arts’ iEar Presents, this screening and discussion will draw on Beckman’s longstanding exploration of how physical action, and our memory of it, constructs cultural images and influences both our behavior and perception of reality. Visit EMPAC Theater at 2:15pm on March 28.
item.author: Community

item.title: OGE Seeking Nominations for Faculty Advising Award
item.description: The Office of Graduate Education is seeking nominations for the Faculty Advising Award for Graduate Education. The award recognizes faculty members whose advising has had a meaningful and lasting impact on graduate students. Nomination letters (max. 2 pages) should provide specific examples of excellence in mentoring and student development, fostering a positive and productive research or academic environment, and the overall impact of the nominee’s advising. Submit to madige@rpi.edu by April 1.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics & Astronomy Colloquium: Dr. Long Ju, Massachusetts Institute of Technology
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: “Emergent Quantum Phenomena in Graphene: Condensed Matter Physics Through a Piece of Carbon”
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Miguel Morales from Flatiron Institute
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Ab-Initio Quantum Embedding” by Dr. Miguel Morales. He is a Research Scientist at the Center for Computational Quantum Physics, Flatiron Institute. His research is focused on the development, implementation, and application of quantum Monte Carlo methods for the study of quantum many-body problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 27: RPI Digital Asset Management (DAM) System
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday March 27 from 11am to 12pm, for a live webinar introducing Rensselaer’s Digital Asset Management System. Discover how to access campus‑wide photography, multimedia files, and institutional assets—all in one place. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: Fidelity Confidential Consultation (In Person)
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 25, 2026 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM - Schedule a confidential consultation to help you become better informed and more confident about your workplace retirement savings plan benefits. To schedule an appointment, call 1-800-642-7131, Monday through Friday, 8:00 am to midnight, or online at https://nb.fidelity.com/public/nbpreloginnav/rpi/microsite/#/tem/meet-with-us
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar: Considering Brave Spaces vs. Safe Spaces in Discussions
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 24, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - In this workshop we will look at the strategies around building brave spaces versus safe spaces and what that means in situations when in a learning environment with a group of peers discussing challenging topics. Maureen Fodera (Office of Undergraduate Education) will be presenting this workshop. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop- Book Discussion: “The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People: Powerful Lessons in Personal Change”-Virtual
item.description:
Join an instructor-led discussion of Stephen R. Covey’s incredibly successful book (available in Percipio). It offers a revolutionary program to breaking the patterns of self-defeating behavior that keep us from achieving our goals and reaching our fullest potential and describes how to replace them with a principle-focused approach to problem-solving.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
